heading: Date Search and Sort
body:
The following query shows how to run a [compound](compound-ref)
operator query and sort the results by a date field. It uses the following
operators:

- [wildcard](wildcard-ref) operator to search for movie titles that begin
  with `Summer`.

- [near](near-ref) operator to search for movies that were released in
  and about five months before or after April 18, 2014.

  > **Note:** When you use `pivot` on a date field, its unit of measure is
  > in milliseconds. MongoDB Search calculates a score for each document based
  > on how close the date field is to the specified date. To learn
  > more, see [near](near-ref).

The query uses the following pipeline stages:

- [$search](pipe.$search) stage to search the `title` and `released`
  fields and then sort the results by the `released` field in
  descending order.

- [$limit](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/limit) stage to limit the output to `5` results.

- [$project](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/project) stage to:

  - Exclude all fields except `title` and `released`.

  - Add a field named `score`.
